message:
style(rebase-recovery): clippy cleanup — expand doc comment on
should_allow_recovery, use `?` on Option instead of let-else

Two tiny clippy-surfaced items on `should_allow_recovery`, both
reformatting:

1. The one-line doc was "Return `Some(RecoveryReason)` if …" which
   reads as a prose fragment without a subject. Split it into a
   short summary ("Check whether a recovery unblock should fire
   for this pack/pattern in this cwd.") plus the existing detail
   sentence, so `cargo doc` / hover rendering has a proper first-
   line summary distinct from the body.

2. Replaced the `let Some(name) = pattern_name else { return None;
   };` early-bail with `let name = pattern_name?;`. Same control
   flow — when `pattern_name` is `None` the function returns
   `None` — but `?` on `Option` is the idiomatic form and keeps
   `should_allow_recovery` as an expression-style body instead of
   a statement-style one. `clippy::needless_question_mark` /
   `clippy::manual_let_else` no longer fire on this function.

No behaviour change.
